items.fromString: CC: Filters are saved in session storage
items.toString: CC: Filters are remembered for duration of session

items.fromString:
Filters set on top of views should persist for a users session. Is cleared when they log out of the system.

FE: 2
QA: 1
items.toString:
Filters set on top of views should persist during the users session. Filters are not not persisted when the users session is timed out or the user explicitly logs out. When they log back in, filters will be back to the tables/views normal configuration

Quick filters (the filter search bar) is not included in the above. This is because in general that is to quickly find a specific item, as opposed to filter down to a set of items.

FE: 2
QA: 1

items.fromString: Filters are persisted within a session even if not saved in a view.
items.toString:
* The last state of the filters added to a table is remembered (persisted) as they navigate away and back to the table
** This includes page navigation and page load/reload
** Quick filter (filter search text bar) is not persisted across page navigation/load
* Filter persistence is cleared if the users session times out OR the user explicitly logs out
** Upon logging back in all tables will “revert” to their standard filters (if any) for the defined default view
* Filters do not persist when a user changes views for the table (if supported). Meaning if I am on View 1 and I set a filter. I then change to View 2, the filters will now be based on View 2’s definition not the filters I used in View 1. If I switch back to View 1, I will now have the filters based on View 1, not the previously used filters I temporarily set in View 1 before switching to view 2.

This work item links to "Wiki Page (System Confluence)"

** This includes page navigation and page load/reload
** Quick filter (filter search text bar) is not persisted across page navigation/load
* Filter persistence is cleared if the users session times out OR the user explicitly logs out
** Upon logging back in all tables will “revert” to their standard filters (if any) for the defined default view
* Filters do not persist when a user changes views for the table (if supported). Meaning if I am on View 1 and I set a filter. I then change to View 2, the filters will now be based on View 2’s definition not the filters I used in View 1. If I switch back to View 1, I will now have the filters based on View 1, not the previously used filters I temporarily set in View 1 before switching to view 2.
items.toString:
* The last state of the filters added to a table is remembered (persisted) as they navigate away and back to the table
** This includes page navigation and page load/reload
** Quick filter (filter search text bar) is not persisted across page navigation/load
* Filter persistence is cleared if the users session times out OR the user explicitly logs out
** Upon logging back in all tables will “revert” to their standard filters (if any) for the defined default view
* Filters do not persist when a user changes views for the table (if supported). Meaning if I am on View 1 and I set a filter. I then change to View 2, the filters will now be based on View 2’s definition not the filters I used in View 1. If I switch back to View 1, I will now have the filters based on View 1, not the previously used filters I temporarily set in View 1 before switching to view 2.

Other AC

* AG Table remembers which view is selected when I navigate from the page and come back. The view returns to the default or the “favorite” view if a user logs out and back in.
* We need to consider how these requirements will work when Custom Views are opted-in vs when only System Views are enabled and when neither are enabled.

Other AC - [~accountid:609d89ab9da6d500704daf78] please review and determine if additional stories are need for the below scenarios.

* AG Table remembers which view is selected when I navigate from the page and come back. The view returns to the default or the “favorite” view if a user logs out and back in.
* We need to consider how these requirements will work when Custom Views are opted-in vs when only System Views are enabled and when neither are enabled.
This work item is cloned by PLA-6998

items.fromString: CC: Filters are remembered for duration of session

Filters set on top of views should persist during the users session. Filters are not not persisted when the users session is timed out or the user explicitly logs out. When they log back in, filters will be back to the tables/views normal configuration

Quick filters (the filter search bar) is not included in the above. This is because in general that is to quickly find a specific item, as opposed to filter down to a set of items.

Requirements: [https://cadent.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/FUN/pages/25986695187/User+Table+Views+w+AG+Grid+PI29+30#3.2.-Table-View-Persistence|https://cadent.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/FUN/pages/25986695187/User+Table+Views+w+AG+Grid+PI29+30#3.2.-Table-View-Persistence|smart-link]
items.toString:
Within a session, the last selected view of a user is maintained for a session.

* Example: On the campaigns page, the default view is View A. User switches to view B. In the same session, user navigates to the Audience page. Then the user navigates back to the campaigns page. View B should be active on page load.

Requirements: [https://cadent.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/FUN/pages/25986695187/User+Table+Views+w+AG+Grid+PI29+30#3.2.-Table-View-Persistence|https://cadent.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/FUN/pages/25986695187/User+Table+Views+w+AG+Grid+PI29+30#3.2.-Table-View-Persistence|smart-link]

items.fromString:

** This includes page navigation and page load/reload
** Quick filter (filter search text bar) is not persisted across page navigation/load
* Filter persistence is cleared if the users session times out OR the user explicitly logs out
** Upon logging back in all tables will “revert” to their standard filters (if any) for the defined default view
* Filters do not persist when a user changes views for the table (if supported). Meaning if I am on View 1 and I set a filter. I then change to View 2, the filters will now be based on View 2’s definition not the filters I used in View 1. If I switch back to View 1, I will now have the filters based on View 1, not the previously used filters I temporarily set in View 1 before switching to view 2.

Other AC - [~accountid:609d89ab9da6d500704daf78] please review and determine if additional stories are need for the below scenarios.

* AG Table remembers which view is selected when I navigate from the page and come back. The view returns to the default or the “favorite” view if a user logs out and back in.
* We need to consider how these requirements will work when Custom Views are opted-in vs when only System Views are enabled and when neither are enabled.
